narrative.headline: 2022 Honda Civic: steering dominates the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Steering problems — particularly vibration in the steering wheel at highway speeds — are the most commonly reported issue for this model, with a substantial majority of owner complaints falling in that area. A secondary cluster of complaints covers the driver-assistance suite, where owners describe the adaptive cruise control disengaging without warning and lane-keep assist making erratic lateral corrections, especially near lane markings, curves, and large vehicles. These assist-system behaviors appear across both the forward collision avoidance and lane departure components, suggesting a broader pattern in the sensing or control logic.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report the steering wheel vibrating noticeably while driving at highway speeds, with the condition prompting dealer inspections. · 27 crash · 9 injured

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Owners report the cruise control — whether in adaptive or standard mode — disengaging randomly and refusing to re-engage, with the cruise control icon appearing on the center display during the event. · 4 crash · 5 injured

issues.title: Lane Departure
issues.blurb: Owners report the lane control assist causing the vehicle to wander, pull side to side, follow lane color changes, tug toward curves, and drift toward large trucks at high speed. · 6 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: Owners report the cruise control cutting out unexpectedly in both adaptive and standard modes, with subsequent attempts to restart the system failing and the cruise control icon displaying on screen. · 1 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The fewest at 38 reports (4%), tied to 3 crashes and 1 injury. Some reports mention the traffic sign recognition showing wrong speed data.
